query: Google Cloud Next 2026 agenda sessions networking platform service new product
answer:
# Google Cloud Next 2026: Agenda Sessions, Networking Platform Services & New Products

## Event Overview

Google Cloud Next 2026 takes place **April 22–24, 2026** at the **Mandalay Bay Convention Center, Las Vegas**. The event theme is **"Where big ideas become a reality"** with the opening keynote titled **"The Agentic Cloud."** The conference has shifted focus from AI experimentation to **production-grade agentic AI systems**, enterprise-scale deployment, and infrastructure hardening.

---

## Agenda Structure & Session Formats

The conference runs across three days with the following high-level agenda:

- **Tuesday, April 21**: Badge Pickup (7 AM–10 PM), Customer Engagement Center (2:30–6 PM)
- **Wednesday, April 22**: Opening Keynote (9–10:30 AM), Expo (9 AM–6 PM), Spotlights/Breakouts/Sessions (11 AM–6 PM), Welcome Happy Hour (4:30–6 PM)
- **Thursday, April 23**: Sessions (8 AM–6 PM), Developer Keynote (10:30–11:45 AM), Expo (9 AM–5:30 PM), Next at Night at Allegiant Stadium (7–9:30 PM)
- **Friday, April 24**: Sessions (8:30 AM–2:30 PM), Expo (9 AM–2 PM), Show Closes at 3 PM

### Session Types
- **Keynotes** (75–90 min): Major announcements, products, customer successes
- **Spotlights** (45 min): Keynote-style with live demos and new tech
- **Breakouts** (45 min): Expert-led deep dives and panel discussions
- **Lightning Talks** (20 min): Specific customer/partner/technical use cases
- **Birds of a Feather** (varies): Peer connection by role/industry
- **Developer Meetups** (45 min): 20+ meetups for developer communities
- **Discussion Groups** (30 min): Expert-led Q&A in small groups
- **Lounge Sessions** (30 min): Casual fireside chats
- **Skills Zone Workshops** (45 min): Interactive lab-style workshops
- **Solution Talks** (45 min): Architecture Q&A with Google experts

### Session Topics (from session library filters)
The session library organizes sessions under these topics: **Agents, APIs, App Dev, Applied AI, Architecture, Compute, Data Analytics, Databases, Kubernetes, Migration, Mobile and Web, Multicloud, Networking, Security**, and more.

---

## Networking Platform Service Sessions

### Key Networking Session: BRK3-039 — "Improve AI Developer Velocity with an Agentic Cross-Cloud Network"
- **Format**: Breakout (Advanced Technical)
- **Topic**: Networking
- **Time**: 8:30 AM – 9:15 AM (Day 3 – April 24, 2026)
- **Location**: Lagoon B
- **Description**: *"Vibe coding and AI development thrive on service-centric platforms where networking is invisible, agile, and secure. To accelerate developer velocity, platform teams must evolve their infrastructure strategy. This session will introduce **new service-networking capabilities for Google Kubernetes Engine and Cloud Run** that are designed to meet the unique, high-scale demands of agentic AI. Join us to learn how to build a seamless platform experience that simplifies traffic management, strengthens identity-based security, and provides real-time observability – all while significantly lowering total cost of ownership and empowering developers."*
- **Key Signal**: This session explicitly promises to **"introduce new service-networking capabilities"** for GKE and Cloud Run — a strong indicator of a new product or feature launch.

### Network Security Session: BRK1-102 — "What's New in Network Security"
- **Full Title**: "What's new in network security: Protecting the AI and Agentic era"
- **Format**: Breakout (Introductory)
- **Topic**: Security
- **Time**: 12:30 PM – 1:15 PM (Day 1 – April 22, 2026)
- **Location**: Mandalay Bay L
- **Description**: Covers Google Cloud's newest **Cross-Cloud Network security features**, including AI-powered capabilities for **Cloud Next Generation Firewall**, **DNS Armor**, and **Google Cloud Armor**. Also demonstrates a **new capability** for network security operations assisting with policy generation, recommendation, and simulation.

### Additional Networking-Adjacent Session: "Securing Agent Traffic at Scale: Network Enforcement for the Agentic Enterprise"
- **Session ID**: 3902760
- **Details**: Listed in the session library but full details require authentication. The title strongly suggests content about network-level enforcement for AI agent communications.

---

## New Product Signals & Expected Announcements

### 1. New Service-Networking Capabilities for GKE and Cloud Run
The BRK3-039 session description explicitly states it will **"introduce new service-networking capabilities"** for GKE and Cloud Run designed for agentic AI at high scale. Based on Google Cloud's pre-conference messaging, these likely involve:
- Enhanced **Cloud Service Mesh** integration (already GA, built on Envoy/Istio)
- **Identity-based security** for service-to-service traffic
- **Real-time observability** for AI agent traffic patterns
- Traffic management simplification for agentic workloads

### 2. Envoy-Based Agentic Networking Stack
Google Cloud published a blog post in advance of Next 2026 titled **"The case for Envoy networking in the agentic AI era"** describing:
- Envoy as the **Agent Gateway** supporting MCP (Model Context Protocol), A2A (Agent2Agent), OpenAI-style APIs, JSON-RPC, gRPC
- **MCP session management** via Streamable HTTP (passthrough and aggregating gateway modes)
- **kube-agentic-networking**: A new Kubernetes SIG project for agent networking
- RBAC, mTLS, SPIFFE identities, zero-trust architecture for agent communications
- Integration with **Cloud Service Mesh** as the control plane

### 3. Cross-Cloud Network Enhancements
- **AWS-Google Cloud multicloud networking** collaboration (announced late 2025, currently in preview): combines Google Cloud's Cross-Cloud Interconnect with AWS Interconnect – Multicloud. Azure expected to join in 2026.
- New AI-powered **network security operations** capabilities for policy generation, recommendation, and simulation

### 4. Opening Keynote: "The Agentic Cloud"
- **Speaker**: Thomas Kurian (CEO, Google Cloud), with Sundar Pichai (CEO, Google/Alphabet)
- **Time**: Wednesday April 22, 9:00–10:30 AM
- Expected to feature **major product announcements**, custom silicon news, next-gen Gemini model family updates, and the roadmap for agentic enterprise transformation

### 5. Developer Keynote
- **Speakers**: Brad Calder (VP/GM, Google Cloud Platform), Jeanine Banks (VP/GM Developer Relations)
- **Time**: Thursday April 23, 10:30–11:45 AM
- Focus on new APIs, IDE integrations, and developer tooling live demos

### 6. GKE/Kubernetes Innovations (Pre-announced at KubeCon EU 2026)
- **Autopilot for Standard Clusters**: Autopilot compute classes available per-workload in Standard clusters
- **GKE Inference Gateway**: Advanced inference ingress support
- **llm-d**: Kubernetes-native distributed inference framework (CNCF Sandbox project)
- **DRANET**: DRA driver for networking, managed feature of GKE
- **GKE MCP Server**: Open-source standardized interface for AI agents to interact with Kubernetes
- **Kubernetes Agent Sandbox**: Secure, gVisor-backed isolation for agents

### 7. Infrastructure & Partnerships
- **Google-Intel partnership** (announced April 9, 2026): Multiyear collaboration using Intel Xeon processors and co-developed custom ASIC-based IPUs to offload networking, storage, and security functions
- **Ironwood TPU** (7th generation): Scaling up to 9,216 chips
- **AI Zones**: Specialized infrastructure extensions for AI training and inference

### 8. Security Products
- **Wiz integration** (Google Cloud acquired Wiz): "AI Application Protection Platform"
- **Agentic SOC** strategy for security operations
- **DNS Armor** and **Cloud NGFW** enhancements with AI-powered threat detection

---

## Conference Tracks (Six Primary Tracks)
1. **Agentic AI**: Building and deploying AI agents across enterprise workflows
2. **Cloud Infrastructure**: Compute, networking, storage, hybrid/multi-cloud architecture
3. **Security**: Zero trust, threat detection, identity, compliance
4. **Developer Experiences**: Tools, APIs, frameworks for building on Google Cloud
5. **Startups**: Programming for early-stage companies
6. **SMB Solutions**: Google Cloud for small/mid-sized businesses

---

## Featured Speakers (Subject to Change)
- **Thomas Kurian** – CEO, Google Cloud
- **Sundar Pichai** – CEO, Google and Alphabet
- **Amin Vahdat** – VP Engineering, Google
- **Brad Calder** – VP/GM, Google Cloud Platform
- **Jeanine Banks** – VP/GM Developer Relations
- **Urs Hölzle** – Google Fellow
- **Gerrit Kazmaier** – VP/GM Data & Analytics
- **Stephanie Wong** – Global Lead, Developer Programs

---

## Key Contextual Data Points
- Event expected to draw **30,000+ attendees** (per Deloitte sponsorship materials)
- **Google-managed MCP servers** now in production for AI agents to interact with Google Maps, BigQuery, and other services (announced March 2026)
- Google Cloud partner program refresh in 2026 adds new **"Diamond" tier** with AI-automated partner evaluation
- **UJET/Google Cloud** launched "Google Cloud CCaaS by UJET" for SMB market (April 14, 2026)
- Google Cloud's AI agent trends report (2026) based on **3,466 global executive** insights describes the shift as the "agent leap"
